location_precision.why: Every one of the six findings carries a usable file:line (e.g. 'src/sync.py:14', 'src/report.py:11').

interaction_synthesis.why: Findings are listed independently and no compound failure mode combining two distinct defects is explained—the :39 note about the report only describes that finding's own downstream consequence, not a mechanism arising from another defect's state.

causal_clarity.why: Each finding states the failure mechanism or breaking condition, e.g. mutable default 'seen=[]' is 'shared across all calls...so users synced in one run are wrongly skipped' and email '.split("@")[1] crashes with AttributeError when email is None...and with IndexError for any address lacking "@"'.

location_precision.why: Every one of the six findings carries a usable file:line (e.g. `src/sync.py:14`, `src/report.py:11`).

interaction_synthesis.why: Each finding is described in isolation; even the :39 error-swallowing note only vaguely says the 'report can no longer reflect' failures without mechanistically linking it to the report.py defects.

causal_clarity.why: Every finding states a failure mechanism, e.g. `seen=[]` 'is shared across all calls' causing wrongly skipped duplicates, and email None 'crashes with AttributeError.'

location_precision.why: Every one of the six findings carries a usable file:line (e.g. 'src/sync.py:14', 'src/report.py:7', 'src/report.py:11').

interaction_synthesis.why: The sync.py:39 finding gestures at an interaction — swallowed errors mean 'the failure count/report can no longer reflect them' — but never mechanistically links it to a specific second defect as a compound failure mode.
